subject: Fwd: FW: Draft complaint
body:

From: "Anne Singer" <asinger@samlegal.com>
Subject: FW: Draft complaint
Date: Mon, 05 May 2008 20:34:35 GMT
To: dkiesq@aol.com
Cc: "Steven Storch" <storch@samlegal.com>

 

 

From: Steven Storch 
Sent: Monday, May 05, 2008 4:28 PM
To: 'Darren K. Indyke (dkiesq@aol.com)'
Cc: Anne Singer
Subject: Draft complaint

 

Darren, my secretary Anne Singer will momentarily be forwarding to you
the latest draft. As it now stands, our view is that the case should be
filed in New York State Supreme. There are a variety of reasons but the
main one is that under recent case law I am concerned that a federal
filing will greatly enhance the chances of being preempted by the class
actions now pending. Under some recent law, that could wipe out the
fraudulent inducement to hold claims which are central here. I am ,
however, looking at the Virgin Islands angle.

 

As for the document itself, The language still  needs polishing and
tightening and we are working on that as I send this email.  However, I
do think it sets forth the appropriate approach and contains the facts
which distinguish our case from the others out there.

 

 Also, to the extent we can expand on any of the conversations between
Jeffrey and Spector that could be helpful as well. The causes of action
are limited because under New York law we have to deal with the Martin
Act line of cases pursuant to which essentially any state law claim is
preempted by the Martin Act unless there is a fraudulent or wrongful
intent element. J.p. Morgan will obviously be added as a  defendant when
the merger formally  closes.

 

Also, you will see in the stock section a reference to a conversation
between Jeffrey and Jimmy Cayne. You will have to let me know whether we
can stick with that angle.

 

I am around into this evening if you want to talk and will try calling
you in a bit. 

 

 

Steven G. Storch

Storch Amini & Munves, P.C.
2 Grand Central Tower
140 E. 45th Street
New York, New York 10017
Tel: (212) 490-4100 ext 216
Fax: (212) 490-4208

Confidentiality Notice: The information contained in this e-mail and any
attachments may be legally privileged and confidential. If you have
received this e-mail in error, please notify the sender and permanently
delete the e-mail and any attachments immediately. You should not
retain, copy or use this e-mail or any attachment for any purpose, nor
disclose all or any part of the contents to any person. Thank you.

From:Anne Singer <asinger@samlegal.com>
Subject:FW: Draft complaint
Date:Mon, 05 May 2008 20:34:35 GMT
To:dkiesq@aol.com
Cc:Steven Storch <storch@samlegal.com>

 

 

From: Steven Storch
Sent: Monday, May 05, 2008 4:28 PM
To: 'Darren K. Indyke (dkiesq@aol.com)'
Cc: Anne Singer
Subject: Draft complaint

 

Darren, my secretary Anne Singer will momentarily be forwarding to you the latest draft. As it now stands, our view is that the case should be filed in New York State Supreme. There are a variety of reasons but the main one is that under recent case law I am concerned that a federal filing will greatly enhance the chances of being preempted by the class actions now pending. Under some recent law, that could wipe out the fraudulent inducement to hold claims which are central here. I am , however, looking at the Virgin Islands angle.

 

As for the document itself, The language still  needs polishing and tightening and we are working on that as I send this email.  However, I do think it sets forth the appropriate approach and contains the facts which distinguish our case from the others out there.

 

 Also, to the extent we can expand on any of the conversations between Jeffrey and Spector that could be helpful as well. The causes of action are limited because under New York law we have to deal with the Martin Act line of cases pursuant to which essentially any state law claim is preempted by the Martin Act unless there is a fraudulent or wrongful intent element. J.p. Morgan will obviously be added as a  defendant when the merger formally  closes.

 

Also, you will see in the stock section a reference to a conversation between Jeffrey and Jimmy Cayne. You will have to let me know whether we can stick with that angle.

 

I am around into this evening if you want to talk and will try calling you in a bit.

 

 

Steven G. Storch
